subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Boards & Tiles
- **19** hex territory tiles
- **4** faction reference cards
- **1** rulebook

#### Animal Meeples (per faction)
- **12** herd meeples (Cows, Chickens, Sheep, or Pigs)
- **3** house pieces
- **3** barn pieces

#### Cards
- **11** espionage cards
- **11** mutation cards
- **5** award cards
- Resource cards: Wood, Wheat, Brick, Weapons, Plutonium

#### Tokens
- Nuke tokens (mushroom cloud)
- VP tokens (sheriff star badge)
- Turmoil tokens (burning building)
- Spy tokens (numbered 1, 2, 3 per player)

#### Dice
- **2** white resource dice (Brick, Wheat, Wood, Weapons, Plutonium faces)
- **6** red combat dice (guns, spy hats, animal heads)

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
1. **Hex map** — Arrange hex territory tiles to form the play area. Tiles show farms, factories, forests, quarries, and wheat fields.
2. **Supply piles** — Separate resource cards into 5 piles: Wood, Wheat, Brick, Weapons, Plutonium.
3. **Token supply** — Place nuke tokens, VP tokens, and turmoil tokens in a general supply area.
4. **Espionage deck** — Shuffle espionage cards face-down.
5. **Mutation deck** — Shuffle mutation cards face-down.
6. **Award cards** — Place award cards face-up where all players can see them.
7. **Reference cards** — Place reference cards showing Building Costs and Turn Order where all can see.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Faction Selection
- Each player picks one of 4 factions: **Cows**, **Chickens**, **Sheep**, or **Pigs**.
- Take your faction card and all matching animal meeples and building pieces.

#### Starting Resources
- Each player draws **1 mutation card** (unique power for the game).
- Each player takes their **3 spy tokens** (numbered 1, 2, 3).
- Starting resource cards dealt per setup rules.

#### Initial Placement
- Place starting herds on territories per setup rules.
- Youngest player goes first.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
Each turn has 4 phases in order:

1. **Preparation** — Remove turmoil tokens from your regions and gain new herds.
2. **Actions** — May move or heal each herd.
3. **Resources** — Roll resource dice for resources.
4. **Discard** — Discard down to 7 or fewer resource cards.

**Building:** Players may build during phases 2 through 4.

subtopics.title: Building Costs
subtopics.content:
#### Buildings
- **House** (1 VP) — Costs: Wood + Plutonium + Brick + Wheat
- **Barn** (2 VP) — Costs: Weapons x3 + Brick + Plutonium

#### Military
- **Nuke** — Costs: Weapons + Brick + Plutonium
- **Spy** — Costs: Wood + Wheat

Buildings can be constructed during phases 2-4 of your turn.

subtopics.title: Combat & Nuking
subtopics.content:
#### Combat
- When herds enter a region with enemy herds, combat occurs.
- Roll red combat dice to resolve.
- Defeated herds are removed from the board.

#### Nuking
- Spend a nuke token to devastate a region.
- Place a turmoil token on the nuked region.
- Herds in a nuked region take damage.
- The Nukiest award goes to whoever builds the most nukes.

#### Espionage
- Play spy cards for special effects.
- Use spy tokens to place hidden agents.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Victory Points
- Houses = 1 VP each
- Barns = 2 VP each
- Award cards = 1 VP each (Nukiest, etc.)
- Territory control bonuses

#### Game End
- Game ends when a player reaches the VP threshold or all territories are controlled.
- Highest VP total wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Expand early** — Claim territories before opponents fortify.
- **Resource diversity** — Collect all 5 resource types to keep building options open.
- **Mutation synergy** — Build your strategy around your mutation card's unique power.
- **Spy placement** — Use spies early to gather intelligence and disrupt opponents.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Overextending** — Spreading herds too thin across the map.
- **Ignoring espionage** — Not using spy cards leaves you vulnerable.
- **Premature nuking** — Nukes are more valuable as threats than as weapons early on.
- **Resource hoarding** — Sitting on 7 cards without building wastes tempo.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
- **Build vs stockpile** — Know when to invest resources in buildings vs saving for defense.
- **Nuke timing** — The threat of a nuke is often more powerful than using it.
- **Faction-specific play** — Each mutation card changes optimal strategy significantly.
- **Territory chokepoints** — Control narrow passages between hex clusters.
